page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical Intel Xeon processor cores running at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results place the instance in the average performance tier for most compute and memory operations. The single-core stress-ng score is 1837.99, and the multi-core score is 30097.54. Notably, the PassMark memory score of 2750.54 is categorized in the top 25% (strong), supported by a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 1639.21 GB/sec. Large block memory operations achieve 107.01 GB/sec for reads and 47.28 GB/sec for writes. Database operations perform at an average level, with Redis SET operations handling 26.96 million operations per second.

Due to its high memory density and strong memory performance, the n2-highmem-32 is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and medium-scale data processing pipelines. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. Additionally, while it can handle smaller language model inference, its performance on larger models is limited, as demonstrated by a weak prompt processing speed of 5.88 tokens per second on a 70B model.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. With 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory capabilities, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and a small-block read bandwidth of 1639.21 GB/sec. Other benchmarks, including stress-ng and Redis SET operations, fall within the average tier. The instance is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without the need for local high-speed storage or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong memory performance for databases and caching workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring thirty-two dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of high-bandwidth system memory.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM providing 8 GB per vCPU
Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz base frequency
Strong memory performance with top-tier PassMark memory scores
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and data processing
